summary: Family-local layout-spacing family with one decorative Spacing atom backed by a token scale, a scale multiplier escape hatch, and shared React/Vue adapter parity.
philosophy: Teach Spacing as a decorative layout primitive and keep content structure, grouping, and section meaning outside the component.
Keep the token scale explicit through Spacings so teams avoid ad hoc magic-number margins where a shared spacer is the honest tool.
Treat scale as a deliberate escape hatch for uncommon layout tuning rather than the default way of inventing new spacing sizes.

Keep the family-local metadata honest: Spacing emits stable data-component and data-size values from core, but it is not part of the wave-1 central semantic registry and currently has no dedicated Figma family source.

useWhen: The layout needs an explicit decorative vertical gap between elements and the team wants that gap to come from the shared spacing token scale.
The product wants React and Vue spacing behavior to stay aligned through one core recipe and one shared token vocabulary.
The surrounding composition benefits from a visible spacer primitive instead of many one-off margins spread across sibling elements.
avoidWhen: The layout should be expressed more honestly through parent gap, stack, grid, or section structure rather than a standalone spacer element.
The visual gap would be carrying semantic meaning that should instead come from headings, dividers, lists, or field grouping.
The team expects a dedicated Figma component family or central semantic-registry entry that the current Spacing family does not ship.

automationCovers: Storybook introduction and taxonomy tests in both apps for the atom teaching surface and token-scale documentation.
Repo-wide typecheck and build paths exercise the exported Spacing atom and Spacings constant in both adapters.
Registry generation and link checks keep the structural docs surface aligned with the shipped file tree.
manualReviewRequired: Product-level judgment about whether Spacing is the honest layout tool, versus parent-owned gap, section structure, or semantic separators.
Visual review that larger spacer sizes are not being used to imply hidden hierarchy or compensate for weak layout structure.
A future decision on whether this tiny family needs direct runtime contract tests, or whether its current docs-led coverage is sufficient.
notes: Spacing completed a first-pass accessibility audit focused on decorative-only usage, layout-honesty guidance, and direct runtime contract coverage.
The family now has core recipe tests, a shared spacing contract, dedicated React/Vue adapter tests, and a documented Vue scale-prop parity fix.

openQuestions: Should Spacing gain a dedicated runtime contract or adapter test file, or is docs-led coverage sufficient for a decorative family this small?
Should the local Figma sync eventually gain a dedicated spacing family or token page that maps directly to the shipped 18-size spacing scale, or is the current code-owned token source sufficient?
